description: Add a waveform viewer with time-aligned horizontal canvas layout for I2C simulation visualization

userStories.title: Horizontal auto-layout for nodes
userStories.description: As a user, I want protocol step nodes to be arranged horizontally in a linear pipeline so that the sequence is visually clear and ready for waveform alignment.
acceptanceCriteria: All nodes are positioned at the same y-coordinate (Y = 200)
Nodes are spaced evenly: x = i * (NODE_WIDTH + GAP) where NODE_WIDTH=160, GAP=40
Adding a new node places it at the end of the sequence with correct position
Deleting a node re-indexes and re-positions all remaining nodes
Edges are auto-generated between consecutive nodes using smoothstep type
nodesConnectable={false} prevents manual edge creation
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser: nodes appear in a horizontal row

sharedFiles.reason: modify node layout logic and add/delete handlers

userStories.notes: Layout function: nodes.map((node, i) => ({ ...node, position: { x: i * (NODE_WIDTH + GAP), y: Y } })). Auto-edges: nodes.slice(0,-1).map((node, i) => ({ id: `e-${node.id}-${nodes[i+1].id}`, source: node.id, target: nodes[i+1].id, type: 'smoothstep' })). Set nodesDraggable={false} initially (US-003 will re-enable with constraints). Key files: App.tsx (main layout logic). The existing code uses React Flow with free-form positioning — this story replaces that with computed positions.

userStories.title: Click-to-append sidebar
userStories.description: As a user, I want to click buttons in the sidebar to append protocol steps to the sequence, replacing the drag-and-drop interaction.
acceptanceCriteria: Sidebar shows buttons for each node type (START, SEND_BYTE, RECV_BYTE, STOP)
Clicking a button appends the node to the end of the sequence
New node automatically gets an edge from the previous node
Drag-and-drop from sidebar is removed
START/STOP auto-management still works (sequence must start with START, end with STOP)
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser: clicking sidebar buttons appends nodes correctly

sharedFiles.reason: modify onDrop/add handlers to use click-to-append

userStories.notes: Current Sidebar.tsx uses onDragStart to set drag data, and App.tsx has onDrop handler. Replace with onClick callbacks passed from App.tsx to Sidebar.tsx. The sidebar should call an addNode(type) function that appends to the nodes array, then the layout function from US-001 recomputes positions. Keep the existing node type definitions (START, SEND_BYTE, RECV_BYTE, STOP, REPEATED_START). Preserve START/STOP auto-management logic.

userStories.title: Drag-to-reorder nodes
userStories.description: As a user, I want to drag nodes horizontally to reorder them in the sequence so I can rearrange steps without deleting and re-adding.
acceptanceCriteria: Nodes can be dragged horizontally only (y-axis locked)
Dropping a node between two others inserts it at that position
After reorder, all nodes snap to their correct evenly-spaced positions
Edges are regenerated to reflect the new order
START must remain first, STOP must remain last (cannot be reordered past boundaries)
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser: dragging a node reorders the sequence

sharedFiles.reason: add drag-reorder handler and position snapping

userStories.notes: Re-enable nodesDraggable but use onNodeDrag/onNodeDragStop to constrain y-axis and implement reorder logic. During drag, calculate insertion index from x-position. On drop, reorder the nodes array and re-run the layout function. Lock START at index 0 and STOP at last index. React Flow's onNodeDrag provides the node position — compare against other node positions to determine insertion point.

userStories.title: VCD parser service
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a backend service that parses VCD files and extracts signal change data so the frontend can render waveforms.
acceptanceCriteria: New file backend/app/services/vcd_parser.py with a parse_vcd() function
Uses the vcdvcd Python package to parse VCD files
Extracts signal changes as [time_ps, value] arrays sorted by time
Handles VCD signal hierarchy (e.g., i2c_system_wrapper.dut.scl maps to scl)
Returns timescale, end_time, and per-signal metadata (width, changes)
Returns all available signal names when no filter is specified
vcdvcd is added to requirements.txt
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Use vcdvcd package (pip install vcdvcd). VCD files contain hierarchical signal names like 'i2c_system_wrapper.dut.scl' — use the leaf name for matching. VCD files are small (KB range) so performance is not a concern. Return format: { timescale: str, end_time: int, signals: { name: { width: int, changes: [[time, value], ...] } } }. Normalize timescale to picoseconds. Check existing VCD files in simulation output to understand the exact hierarchy paths.

userStories.title: Waveform signals API endpoint
userStories.description: As a frontend developer, I want a REST endpoint that returns parsed VCD signal data as JSON so I can render waveforms in the browser.
acceptanceCriteria: New endpoint: GET /api/waveform/{waveform_id}/signals
Accepts optional 'signals' query parameter (comma-separated signal names)
When signals param is omitted, returns all available signals
Response format: { timescale, end_time, signals: { [name]: { width, changes } } }
Returns 404 if waveform_id not found
Returns 400 if requested signal name doesn't exist in the VCD
Typecheck passes

sharedFiles.reason: add new GET endpoint alongside existing waveform route

userStories.notes: Add route to simulation.py alongside existing GET /api/waveform/{waveform_id}. Use vcd_path_for(waveform_id) from backend/app/services/waveform.py to locate the VCD file, then call parse_vcd() from US-004. The existing waveform service already handles VCD file lookup and 404 cases — reuse that pattern. Add Pydantic response model for type safety.

userStories.title: Record per-step simulation timing
userStories.description: As a developer, I want each protocol step result to include start_time_ps and end_time_ps so the frontend can align nodes with waveform time ranges.
acceptanceCriteria: Cocotb driver records sim time before and after each step execution using cocotb.utils.get_sim_time(units='ps')
Non-protocol ops (reset, delay) also record timing
Step results in the API response include time_range_ps: [start, end]
RunResponse includes sim_time_total_ps field
Timing values are plausible (start < end, no overlaps between consecutive steps)
Typecheck passes

sharedFiles.reason: modify RunResponse model to include timing fields

userStories.notes: Key files: backend/sim/i2c_driver.py (I2CDriver class), backend/sim/test_runner.py (execute_sequence, _map_protocol_results). Use cocotb.utils.get_sim_time(units='ps') — cocotb 2.0 API. Record timing in execute_sequence() around each step execution. The _map_protocol_results() function maps driver-level TxnResult back to protocol steps — extend it to carry timing. For reset/delay ops, wrap the await with timing capture. The RunResponse Pydantic model in simulation.py needs new fields: each step gets time_range_ps (list of two ints), and top-level sim_time_total_ps. Note: sim files use bare imports since subprocess runs with cwd=sim/.

userStories.title: SVG waveform renderer
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see digital waveforms rendered below the canvas so I can visualize the I2C bus activity.
acceptanceCriteria: New component frontend/src/components/WaveformPanel.tsx renders below the React Flow canvas
New file frontend/src/lib/waveform.ts with fetchWaveformSignals() API call and timeToX() utility
Renders digital square-wave SVG paths from signal change data
Each signal is drawn in its own row with a label on the left
SDA and SCL are shown by default
Panel is collapsible with a toggle button
Panel only appears when simulation results exist (waveform_id is set)
New types added to frontend/src/lib/api.ts for waveform signal data
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser: waveforms render correctly after simulation

sharedFiles.reason: embed WaveformPanel component and pass props
add waveform signal types and fetch function

userStories.notes: SVG path generation: for each signal change [time, value], draw vertical transition then horizontal hold. Use timeToX(time_ps) = LABEL_WIDTH + (time_ps / total_time) * WAVEFORM_WIDTH. Signal labels go in left column (LABEL_WIDTH wide). WaveformPanel sits below the React Flow canvas div, inside the same flex container. Fetch signal data from GET /api/waveform/{id}/signals after simulation completes. The panel should be a new div below the ReactFlow component, not inside it.

userStories.title: Signal selector
userStories.description: As a user, I want to select which signals to display in the waveform panel so I can focus on relevant signals or add debug signals.
acceptanceCriteria: A signal selector UI lists all available signals from the VCD
Users can toggle individual signals on/off
SDA and SCL are enabled by default
Additional signals (busy, done, ack_error, state, etc.) can be toggled on
Signal order in the panel matches the selection order
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser: toggling signals adds/removes waveform rows
userStories.notes: Add signal selector UI to WaveformPanel (e.g., a row of toggle chips/checkboxes above the waveform area). Store selected signals in local state. When selection changes, filter the rendered signal rows. The API already returns all signals when no filter param is specified — use the signal names from the response to populate the selector.

userStories.title: Step region overlays
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see semi-transparent colored regions on the waveform that correspond to each protocol step so I can correlate bus activity with steps.
acceptanceCriteria: Semi-transparent colored rectangles overlay the waveform for each step's time range
Each step type has a distinct color matching the node color scheme
Step labels are shown within or above the overlay regions
Overlays span the full height of the waveform area
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser: colored regions appear aligned with step time ranges

sharedFiles.reason: add overlay rendering using step timing data

userStories.notes: Use SVG <rect> elements with opacity ~0.15 behind the waveform paths. Color map by step type: START=blue, SEND_BYTE=green, RECV_BYTE=orange, STOP=red (match existing node styling). Use the same timeToX() function to position rects. Step timing comes from the simulation result (time_range_ps per step from US-006). Labels can be <text> elements centered in each rect.

userStories.title: Post-simulation node realignment
userStories.description: As a user, I want nodes to resize and reposition after simulation to match their actual time ranges so that nodes and waveforms are visually aligned.
acceptanceCriteria: After simulation, each node's x-position is set to timeToX(step.start_time_ps)
Each node's width is set proportional to its time range duration
Strict linear scaling is used (no minimum width clamp)
Narrow nodes (short time steps like START/STOP) show full details on hover via tooltip
timeToX() function is shared between node layout and waveform rendering (imported from lib/waveform.ts)
Before simulation (or on reset), nodes revert to evenly-spaced default layout
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser: nodes align with waveform regions after simulation

sharedFiles.reason: add post-simulation relayout logic using step timing
timeToX may need adjustment for shared use with node layout

userStories.notes: After simulation: node[i].position.x = timeToX(step[i].start_time_ps), node[i].style.width = timeToX(step[i].end_time_ps) - timeToX(step[i].start_time_ps). Import timeToX from lib/waveform.ts. For narrow nodes, add a title attribute or Tooltip component showing full step details. When user clears results or starts new sequence, revert to default evenly-spaced layout from US-001.

userStories.title: Viewport synchronization
userStories.description: As a user, I want the waveform panel to stay aligned with the canvas when I pan or zoom so that the correspondence between nodes and waveforms is maintained.
acceptanceCriteria: WaveformPanel receives React Flow viewport transform (x, y, zoom)
Pan and zoom transforms are applied to the waveform SVG viewport
Horizontal scrolling keeps nodes and waveform regions aligned
Zoom level changes scale the waveform proportionally
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser: panning/zooming canvas keeps waveform aligned
userStories.notes: Use React Flow's useViewport() hook or onViewportChange callback to get { x, y, zoom }. Pass to WaveformPanel. Apply transform to SVG content group: <g transform={`translate(${viewport.x}, 0) scale(${viewport.zoom})`}>. Only sync horizontal (x) and zoom — vertical pan is independent. Consider throttling viewport updates if performance is an issue.

userStories.title: Hover/click cross-highlighting
userStories.description: As a user, I want hovering over a node to highlight the corresponding waveform region (and vice versa) so I can quickly correlate steps with bus activity.
acceptanceCriteria: Shared state (hoveredStepIndex, selectedStepIndex) is managed in App.tsx
Hovering a node highlights the corresponding waveform step overlay
Hovering a waveform step region highlights the corresponding node
Clicking selects a step (persistent highlight until another is clicked)
Highlight style is visually distinct (e.g., brighter overlay, node border glow)
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser: hover/click cross-highlighting works bidirectionally

sharedFiles.reason: add hover/click handlers and highlight styling

userStories.notes: State: { hoveredStepIndex: number | null, selectedStepIndex: number | null }. Lift to App.tsx. Pass to WaveformPanel and node components. For nodes: add onMouseEnter/onMouseLeave handlers via custom node components. For waveform: add handlers on step overlay <rect> elements. Highlight: increase overlay opacity to ~0.3 on hover, add CSS class to node (e.g., ring-2 ring-blue-400). Selected = persistent version of hover highlight.
